message:
fix(fcp-cbor, fcp-auth-schema): enforce canonical 128-depth recursion limit on parse

The canonicalizer refuses inputs deeper than `MAX_CANONICALIZATION_DEPTH
= 128`, but callers that decoded untrusted CBOR into a
`ciborium::Value` before handing it to the canonicalizer were relying
on `ciborium`'s library-default recursion limit (256). That gap let
an adversarial issuer build a 129..=256-deep CBOR tree that:

  (a) decoded successfully into a fully-allocated `Value` tree
      (allocation bomb),
  (b) was only then rejected as over-depth by the canonicalizer,
      wasting the memory + CPU the decode already spent.

Fix: expose the canonical bound and tie the deserializer to it.

## crates/fcp-cbor/src/lib.rs

- `MAX_CANONICALIZATION_DEPTH` becomes `pub const` (was crate-private).
- `MAX_DESERIALIZATION_RECURSION_LIMIT` becomes `pub const`, documented
  as the right input to
  `ciborium::de::from_reader_with_recursion_limit`. Defined equal to
  `MAX_CANONICALIZATION_DEPTH` (was `CANONICALIZATION_DEPTH + 1`), so
  parse-time refusal matches canonicalize-time refusal exactly.
- `map_cbor_deserialize_error`'s depth field now reports
  `MAX_DESERIALIZATION_RECURSION_LIMIT + 1` (i.e., "one past the
  accepted limit") instead of the old off-by-one value — callers
  receiving `SerializationError::DepthExceeded { depth, max }` now see
  a (depth, max) pair where `depth > max`.
- Existing "over-limit" test updated to match the new wire-out field
  (`depth == MAX_CANONICALIZATION_DEPTH + 1`).
- New regression test `decoder_limit_matches_canonical_depth_boundary`:
    * builds an exactly-`MAX_CANONICALIZATION_DEPTH`-deep singleton
      array tree — must decode cleanly (boundary-inclusive),
    * builds a `MAX_CANONICALIZATION_DEPTH + 1`-deep tree — must fail
      with `SerializationError::DepthExceeded { depth:
      MAX_CANONICALIZATION_DEPTH + 1, max: MAX_CANONICALIZATION_DEPTH }`
      straight from the parser, not the canonicalizer.

## crates/fcp-auth-schema/src/claims.rs

`AuthClaims::from_canonical_cbor` now uses
`ciborium::de::from_reader_with_recursion_limit(&mut cursor,
fcp_cbor::MAX_DESERIALIZATION_RECURSION_LIMIT)` instead of
`ciborium::from_reader`. Same canonical-form validation path
afterwards (position-at-EOF check, per-label decode), but the initial
Value construction now fails at 129 levels of nesting, which is the
boundary the canonicalizer applies.

Comment explains the rationale inline so the magic constant's purpose
is obvious next time an agent refactors this function.

No public surface changes. No wire format changes — a well-formed
input that was accepted before is still accepted; only previously
"decodes then gets rejected" trees now fail earlier with the same
error code.
